programmes.show_description: On a journey to find the lost and forgotten recipes of India, Alex finds herself at Asia's biggest spice market.
From the land of the hallowed Camp Nou stadium to the Catalan capital boasting amazing seafood, Barcelona is a city with many shades to it.
The host comes to know many interesting facts and stories about the pyramids of Giza, when she visits them.
From the Perperikon fort to the Sofia Central Market, Catriona explores the cultural treasures of Sofia, the capital of Bulgaria.
Travelling along the scenic Manali-Leh highway, Alex stops and camps at Korzok village, where she explores the simple ways of life.
On the map of Mexico, Tlaquepaque looks like just another suburb of Guadalajara, but Charlie knows that there is more to this town than meets the eye.
From a rich lamb curry to a hearty pulao made with rare mushrooms, Alex learns to make the secret recipes of a local family in Delhi.
Barcelona was the canvas of the world-renowned architect, Antoni Gaudi, who branded the city with his fantastical creations.
While exploring Alexandria's iconic attractions, the host experiences the sharp contrast of history and modernity that characterizes the city.
Exploring Brussels makes Catriona realize that it's the city where historic meets hip, eccentric meets futuristic and traditional meets cosmopolitan.
From the divine Thikse monastery to the serene Pangong Lake, Alex Outhwaite soaks in the Zen at Leh.
Charlie's journey across his homeland brings him to the birthplace of tequila, the iconic Mexican spirit to understand what makes it irresistible.
From papad ka paratha to lehsun ki kheer, Chef Aditya Jaimini shows Alex Outhwaite how to make some of the unusual dishes from Indian cuisine.
While checking out the art scene of Madrid and enjoying the liveliness at a local market, Alex is engulfed by the energy of this young European city..
The respect accorded to women in ancient Egypt is evident in almost every aspect of the civilization from the religious beliefs to social customs.
Cologne is all things romantic from the chocolate museum to its perfume shop. It is a city in love with life, one that knows how to have a good time.
At Nubra Valley, Alex participates in the local dance, and then sets off for a ride on a Bactrian camel across the Hunder sand dunes.
From the towering Peña de Bernal monolith to the Queretaro wine region, Charlie visits some of Mexico's lesser-known cultural gems.
Alex uncovers the secret behind the irresistible Shahi Dal at Amritsar's iconic eating joint, Kesar Da Dhaba.
Alex finds a piece of Africa called Bioparc in Spain's third largest city, Valencia.
Sabrina Chakici marvels at the architectural wonders of Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan, while also checking out souvenirs at the local bazaar.
From soothing landscapes to relaxing massages, Indeya Passfield explores all that Kandy has to offer in terms of rejuvenation.
Holiday Inspiration. Discover the perfect holiday for you, featuring the hottest destinations and exclusive deals to make your next holiday the best one.
Charlie Rubio visits one of the many 'indigenous paradises' across Mexico, where he gets a chance to visit a hidden heron sanctuary on an island.
The cuisine of Punjab is rich in flavour because it is farm-to-fork and still employs traditional cooking methods such as cooking in a charcoal oven.
Be it Europe's largest marine aquarium or a bullfighting ring, Alex finds Spain's modern elegance and old-world charm meeting in Valencia.
Sabrina Chakici visits the city of Ganja in Azerbaijan, where she uncovers the history at sights like Heyder Aliyev Park and Mausoleum Nizami.
The host visits Dubai's very own tropical rainforest and a wildlife sanctuary that is home to wild flamingos.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget.
In Monterrey, one of Mexico's 'Pueblo Magicos', Charlie heads straight to a canyon, where the locals go to have 'beer and barbecue' picnics.
Alex Outhwaite meets a Nawab in Lucknow, who shows her how to make Gosht Mawazad, a decadent Awadhi dish with mutton and dry fruits.
Jamie Srubis tries out basic Ayurvedic spa treatments such as the Four-Hands Synchronous Massage at the Vasundhara Sarovar Premiere Resort in Kerala.
In Sheki, Sabrina Chakici learns about the art form, Shabaka, indulges in a crude oil spa treatment and explores Khinalug Village.
From the stories of famous Egyptian pharaohs to Greco-Roman beliefs, the ancient temples of Egypt contain mysteries waiting to unfold.
From stumbling upon a statue of Guru Rinpoche to attending a local festival at a monastery, Alex explores the influence of Buddhism in Zanskar Valley.
What began as an adventurous getaway in Santiago unexpectedly transforms into a spiritually moving experience for Charlie!
The pre-Mughal Islamic architecture of Champaner and the Rajput temples of Pavagadh have earned them the title, "University of Architecture".
Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.
Travel show highlighting places to go, the history of Britain, cookery and news.
From a magical shikara ride on Dal Lake to an elaborate Kashmiri Wazwan, Alex experiences "paradise on Earth" in Srinagar.
In Cuatro Ciénegas, Charlie finds himself uncovering the wonders of the destination, one more surreal than the other.
Alex Outhwaite meets the descendants of the royal families of Hyderabad and Lucknow, and they tell her about the culture and cuisine of both kingdoms.
The pre-Mughal Islamic architecture of Champaner and the Rajput temples of Pavagadh have earned them the title, "University of Architecture".
Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.
The central plains of Sri Lanka are dotted with crumbling temples, lost cities and ruins of sacred Buddhist sites are few of the sites now ranking as national treasures.
From a magical shikara ride on Dal Lake to an elaborate Kashmiri Wazwan, Alex experiences "paradise on Earth" in Srinagar.
In Cuatro Ciénegas, Charlie finds himself uncovering the wonders of the destination, one more surreal than the other.
Alex Outhwaite meets the descendants of the royal families of Hyderabad and Lucknow, and they tell her about the culture and cuisine of both kingdoms.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi explores Monts De Cristal National Park, and also learns about prehistoric settlements and Bwiti religion at Lope National Park
Sabrina Chakici visits Manchester, the city of football and fashion, the city of heritage and hedonism, the city of the industrial revolution.
Join Iona as she begins her 50 states journey in Florida, where familiar coastlines and hidden corners reveal fresh stories.
From a canoe ride on the Danube river to new flavours at the local market, Heather explores the sassy side of Serbia's second largest city.
In the ancient city of Anuradhapura, home to the sapling of the tree under which Buddha attained Enlightenment, Mohit finds deep bliss.
From chicken with bamboo shoots to a banana stem dish called Posola Musar Ali Khan, Alex learns to make some popular dishes of Assam.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi learns about the rare species of Lope National Park, and the research and conservation done to preserve them.
Sabrina Chakici gets up, close and personal with Budapest, the capital of Hungary, and she gets to know about its history and character.
Join Iona as she begins her 50 states journey in Florida, where familiar coastlines and hidden corners reveal fresh stories.
While exploring the ruins of a powerful Roman settlement in Viminacium, Heather stumbles upon the remains of a million-year-old female woolly mammoth.
The stone-walled dungeons of Fort Santiago, in Manila, were once witnesses of the most gruesome events in Philippine history.
While learning to prepare a Naga pork delicacy, Alex gets an understanding of the different dishes and cooking styles of Nagaland's tribes.
In Libreville, Emeline Nsingi Nkosi tries bush meat, explores the local fashion at a vibrant market and hunts for souvenirs at an artisan village.
Looking for a break from the chaos and stress of city life, Asmita decides to visit Haridwar, a place which she had last visited with her family.
Nashville
Heather explores Serbia's largest national park, the Djerdap National Park, where she finds herself transfixed with the astounding Iron Gates gorge.
Elisha heads to Bohol, in Philippines, to see the unique Chocolate Hills consisting of 1268 hills that are mysteriously identical in shape.
From traditional smoked pork in Nagaland to frog meat in Shillong, Alex finds out the secrets of tribal cuisine in Nagaland.
The monuments of Agra and Fatehpur Sikri tell stories of the Mughal opulence and their love for the finer things in life.
Travel lover Larissa D'Sa comes to Rishikesh looking for a relaxing getaway, and loses herself in its natural beauty.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget.
Heather visits the site of the first mass escape in occupied Europe at Ni, where she comes to know the story of an impossibly daring prison break.
The Boracay island of Philippines has all the right ingredients for a fun day: sugar-white beaches, water adventures and live Filipino music.
While cooking the local fish of the Jaintia region of Meghalaya, Alex also gets acquainted with the culinary heritage of Sylhet in Bangladesh.
Jamie Srubis tries many rejuvenating spa treatments belonging to Ayurveda and Traditional Chinese Medicine, at Mines Wellness Resort in Malaysia.
Be it stave churches, Viking villages or dramatic fjords, Norway's marvelous heritage reveals its rich history.
Holiday Inspiration. Discover the perfect holiday for you, featuring the hottest destinations and exclusive deals to make your next holiday the best one.
From the murals of a monastery in Despotovac to a 19th century experience in Zlatibor, Heather soaks in Serbia's cultural vivacity.
At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does
In Kolkata, Alex meets Rajiv Neogi and Siddhartha Bose, food experts who introduce her to some of the forgotten dishes of Bengali cuisine.
Turkey is a perfect place for some adventure and old charm with rugged landscapes, carved castles and varied mezze. Hot air balloon is an attractive sport done by all visitor.
Here's your cheat sheet to the most unique water-based experiences in the Maldives like feeding stingrays.
At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does
Heather visits the stunning Uvac canyons, where she encounters the rare griffon vulture and takes a boat ride to a secret cave.
While exploring Belgrade, Heather goes on an underground tour where she stumbles onto some mysterious old tunnels.
At the monumental Lion Paws Gate of a 5th century Sri Lankan fortress, full of centuries-old rock paintings, Mohit is all set to relive a bygone era.
Sabrina Chakici explores the Baroque splendor and musical heritage of Salzburg, the cultural capital of Austria.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi soaks in the stunning biodiversity of Loango National Park, while spotting wildlife.
Join Iona as she begins her 50 states journey in Florida, where familiar coastlines and hidden corners reveal fresh stories.
From a traditional Awadhi korma to Rewa's famous Mutton Bandhwesh, Alex explores the vast and complex culinary culture of India's royal kitchens.
